title: Severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) real-time RT-PCR CCDC-ORF1ab 2020
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5gpju","text":"A real-time RT-PCR designed to amplify a portion of the ORF1ab gene of sequences from the Severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aluk9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1712d","text":"The probe and primers were published by the China CDC and are now included in the WHO in-house assay document.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ja1d","text":"This test has been modified for use with our standard reagents and instrumentation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a382d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ht45","text":"This test has identified clinical positive cases of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5vi8s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dq8e6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: SuperScript™ III Platinum™ One-Step qRT-PCR Kit Life Technologies Catalog #11732088

Synthetic controls are produced using the binary synthetic template oligonucleotide positive control for in-house diagnostic real-time RT-PCR method. The oligonucleotide sequences required to make controls for this assay are: Probe control: AAAATAATACGACTCACTATAGGGTGAAGAGAATCCACAAGGAATTGAACCGTCTGCGGTATGTGGAAAGGTTATGGACAGTGTTCAGCAGGTCCTGTTGAAAA Primer control: AAAATAATACGACTCACTATAGGGCCCTGTGGGTTTTACACTTAAATGATCTGGCACGGGACCCTCCAATCAGCTGATGCACAATCGTAAAA

Assay has been designed to be used on both a Rotor-Gene 6000 / Rotor-Gene Q 5-plex using 100-place rotor discs and a ABI 7500 Fast real-time machine. Total reaction volume is 20µL. Prepare sufficient for number of reaction plus a 'dead volume' usually 2 extra. Adjust as necessary if using a robotic dispenser. %s %s Dispense 15µl to each reaction well. Add 5µl of template, extracted RNA, controls or NTC (nuclease-free water). Total reaction volume is 20µl.

The definition used for a satisfactory positive result from a real-time fluorogenic PCR should include each of the following: A sigmoidal curve – the trace travels horizontally, curves upward, continues in an exponential rise and followed by a curve towards a horizontal plateau phase A suitable level of fluorescence intensity as measured in comparison to a positive control (y-axis) A defined threshold cycle (C T ) value which the fluorescent curve has clearly exceeded (Fig.1 arrow) and which sits early in the log-linear phase and is <40 cycles A flat or non-sigmoidal curve or a curve that crosses the threshold with a C T value >40 cycles is considered a negative result NTCs should not produce a curve. Figure 1. Examples of satisfactory sigmoidal amplification curve shape when considering an assay’s fluorescent signal output. The crossing point or threshold cycle (C T ) is indicated (yellow arrow); it is the value at which fluorescence levels surpass a predefined (usually set during validation, or arbitrary) threshold level as shown in this normalized linear scale depiction. LP-log-linear phase of signal generated during the exponential part of the PCR amplification; TP-a slowing of the amplification and accompanying fluorescence signal marks the transition phase; PP-the plateau phase is reached when there is little or no increase in fluorescent signal despite continued cycling.
creator: Judy A Northill
